\documentclass{siddur}

% Custom page numbering to yield 146.a, 146.b, etc.
\renewcommand{\thepage}{146.\alph{page}}

% --- MACRO DEFINITIONS ---
\newcommand{\blockA}{%
  \begin{hebrew}
  שְׁמַע וּבִרְכוֹתֶיהָ \\
SH'MA UVIRCHOTEHA — SH'MA AND ITS BLESSINGS
  \end{hebrew}%
}

\newcommand{\blockB}{%
  \begin{hebrew}
  בָּרְכוּ אֶת יְיָ הַמְבֹרָךְ! \\
בָּרוּךְ יְיָ הַמְבֹרָךְ \\
לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד!
  \end{hebrew}%
}

\newcommand{\blockC}{%
  BAR’CHU et Adonai hamvorach! \\
Baruch Adonai ham'vorach \\
l'olam va-ed!%
}

\newcommand{\blockD}{%
  PRAISE ADONAI to whom praise is due forever! \\
Praised be Adonai to whom praise is due, \\
now and forever!%
}

\newcommand{\blockE}{%
  The Shma is one of the prayers one may recite in any language. M. Sotah 7:1 \\
For those who choose: The prayer leader at the word Bar'chu (the call to worship) bends \\
the knees and bows from the waist, and at » Adonai stands straight. »77 Baruch Adonai is the \\
communal response, whereupon the community repeats the choreography of the first line.%
}
